hero.title: Explainable multimodal emotion analysis from children's drawings
hero.subtitle: This prototype evaluates children's drawings and, when available, accompanying text with an EfficientNet-B0 and BERTurk based architecture trained on the KIDO dataset. Its purpose is to provide an explainable decision-support flow that can assist expert review.
highlights: A multimodal workflow built on 10,856 examples from the KIDO dataset
Explainable outputs with Grad-CAM and token importance scoring
A research prototype delivered through a FastAPI service and React interface

home.panelTitle: The report distilled into one screen
home.panelSubtitle: The system processes the drawing image together with optional text to predict emotion, while gender classification is handled as an auxiliary task to strengthen shared representation learning.
panelFlow: The drawing is accepted as input and prepared at 224x224 resolution.
If text is available, it is tokenized with BERT up to a maximum length of 128 tokens.
EfficientNet-B0 and BERTurk outputs are fused, then emotion and auxiliary gender heads are evaluated.
home.quickCardOneText: EfficientNet-B0 is used for the visual stream, while dbmdz/bert-base-turkish-cased powers the text stream.
home.quickCardTwoText: Grad-CAM heatmaps and token importance scores make model decisions more transparent.

stats.label: Total samples
stats.note: Emotion and gender labels from KIDO were merged into a single master table.

stats.label: Train / test split
stats.note: The original split reported in the project report was preserved.

stats.label: Train / validation
stats.note: The training partition was split again during experimentation.

stats.label: Primary success threshold
stats.note: The proposal's 85% accuracy target for emotion classification was surpassed.

home.workflowIntro: The home page summarizes the experimental pipeline from the report as a clear product flow.

workflowSteps.title: Data preparation
workflowSteps.body: Emotion and gender CSV files are merged via id and split fields, then drawing paths and Turkish-first text fields are added to the working dataset.

workflowSteps.title: Multimodal inference
workflowSteps.body: Visual features are extracted with EfficientNet-B0, textual features with BERTurk, and fused representations feed the emotion and auxiliary gender heads.

workflowSteps.title: Explanation and delivery
workflowSteps.body: Outputs are presented together with confidence scores, Grad-CAM maps, and token importance scores when text is available.

home.modulesTitle: Model, service, and interface were designed together
home.modulesIntro: According to the report, the project did not stop at a training run; it also produced a reproducible experimentation layer and a user-facing prototype.

modules.title: Data and experiment layer
modules.body: The master_emotion_gender.csv generation flow, experiment records, and reproducible reporting under artifacts/report_run were prepared.

modules.title: Multimodal core
modules.body: The EfficientNet-B0 and BERTurk architecture treats emotion as the primary task and gender as the auxiliary task.

modules.title: Explainability module
modules.body: Visual focus regions are shown with Grad-CAM, while textual contribution is exposed through token-level importance scores.

modules.title: Service and presentation
modules.body: The FastAPI inference service and React interface turn the research output into an interactive prototype.

home.outcomesIntro: The project outcome is not only a trained model; it is an end-to-end experimentation and presentation chain.
outcomes: Reproducible scripts for training, evaluation, and reporting
Prototype tools for single-sample prediction and explanation generation
A local service layer built around the FastAPI /api/predict endpoint
A React-based front end that exposes the workflow to expert users
home.ethicsText: As stated in the report, this system is not a clinical diagnosis tool. It should be used as an explainable decision-support prototype that assists expert judgment.

analysis.title: Run Analysis
analysis.subtitle: Upload a drawing to generate an inference report.
analysis.drop: Drop image here or click to upload
analysis.disclaimer: This tool is a prototype intended for academic research. Its outputs are probabilistic and must not be interpreted as a clinical diagnosis. Professional assessment should always involve qualified experts.
analysis.textPlaceholder: Enter any available description about the drawing...
analysis.noExplanation: No explanation was returned by the backend for this result.
analysis.backendHint: Make sure the Python API server is running: uvicorn api_server:app --port 8000

about.objectiveText: This project developed an explainable artificial intelligence system capable of detecting emotional states from children's drawings. While the original goal focused on emotion analysis from drawings, the final system expanded into a multimodal architecture that jointly processes the drawing image and, when available, the accompanying text.

facts.label: Project title
facts.value: Emotion State Analysis with Deep Learning from Children's Drawings

facts.label: Institution
facts.value: Nigde Omer Halisdemir University, Computer Engineering

goals: Build a workable data structure that combines drawing images with paired text
Develop a deep learning model that performs emotion classification
Deliver a prototype system that presents the results to users
about.dataIntro: The project used the KIDO dataset. Emotion and gender labels were merged through shared sample identifiers to produce the master_emotion_gender.csv file that served as the main data source.
dataPoints: A total of 10,856 samples were used.
The original data split consists of 9,226 training and 1,630 test samples.
During experimentation, the training partition was further divided into 7,843 training and 1,383 validation samples.
Emotion classes are Happiness and Sadness, while the auxiliary task uses Female and Male.
Text content was incorporated with a Turkish-first preference.
about.methodologyIntro: The report describes the multimodal and multi-task design across four main areas.

methodologyCards.title: General architecture
items: EfficientNet-B0 is used as the visual backbone.
dbmdz/bert-base-turkish-cased is used as the text backbone.
Visual and textual embeddings are fused in a shared layer.
Separate classification heads are defined for emotion and gender.

methodologyCards.title: Pre-processing
items: Random horizontal flips and rotations are applied.
Texts are tokenized with a maximum sequence length of 128.

methodologyCards.title: Training setup
items: The model was trained for 5 epochs with batch size 16 and learning rate 0.0001.
AdamW was used with weight decay 0.0001 and validation ratio 0.15.
The random seed was fixed at 42.
Gender remained an auxiliary task while emotion stayed the main evaluation axis.

methodologyCards.title: Explainability
items: Grad-CAM highlights the visual regions that drive the model decision.
Token importance analysis scores the effect of words and subwords in text input.
The system returns not only labels, but also the rationale behind the decision.

about.resultsIntro: Emotion classification was reported as the primary task and exceeded the proposal's 85% accuracy target.
about.emotionResultsText: Balanced separation was achieved across the Happiness and Sadness classes.
about.genderResultsText: This task was not the primary objective; it was included to strengthen shared representation learning.
deliverables: A trained multimodal model, best-weight checkpoints, and retraining scripts
An explainability layer that produces Grad-CAM visuals and token importance scores
A FastAPI service layer that returns predictions, confidence scores, explanations, and heatmaps
A React-based web interface and desktop prototype approach for user-facing interaction
limitations: The dataset is limited to only two emotion classes.
Model evaluation was performed on a single dataset.
The auxiliary gender task raises ethical concerns and must be handled carefully.
The system must not be used for clinical diagnosis.
futureWork: Increase the number of emotion classes
Reassess auxiliary tasks from ethical and scientific perspectives
Select the best model directly with emotion-centered criteria
Perform external validation on additional datasets
Run human-centered evaluation studies with expert feedback
Mature the web interface for field-oriented usage scenarios
about.disclaimer: As explicitly stated in the report, this system is not a clinical diagnosis tool; it should be used to support expert evaluation.
